mechanism_narrative: A low or falling federal funds rate raises equity valuations by lowering the discount rate on expected cash flows, so a sustained easing path predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in a majority of periods when FEDFUNDS is falling for at least 2 consecutive monthly observations, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation accelerates above the recent trend while policy rates remain near zero, real-rate compression is interpreted as liquidity support and S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rate rises above its prior 3-month average while FEDFUNDS stays below 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the triggered set does not beat unconditional 5-day SPX returns by at least 1 percentage point on average, or if more than 60% of triggered observations have negative 5-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate indicates deteriorating household income and demand, so increases in UNRATE predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of months meeting the trigger, or if the sign of the coefficient on UNRATE changes persistently across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in retail sales and industrial production signals broad real-demand strength, which supports earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive changes in RSAFS and INDPRO fail to outperform single-variable signals, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of joint-strength observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts are an early demand-cycle indicator; a sharp decline in HOUST signals weakening construction and consumer demand, which leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 10% versus its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 55% over any 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production with stable payroll growth signals supply-side improvement and higher near-term profit margins, so the S&P 500 should rise over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises while PAYEMS growth remains nonnegative for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if combined upturns in INDPRO and PAYEMS do not produce positive 60-day forward returns more often than not, or if the effect disappears when PAYEMS growth turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll growth stalls and unemployment rises together, labor income momentum breaks down and equity earnings expectations deteriorate, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth is negative and UNRATE increases in the same monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-deterioration trigger does not precede negative 5-day returns, or if the signal is not stronger than using UNRATE alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime, strong retail sales alongside falling housing starts indicates demand is rotating away from rate-sensitive sectors toward current consumption, which supports S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation remains above its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if, during high inflation, the combination of stronger RSAFS and weaker HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign reverses when CPI inflation is elevated.
